original_hypothesis.title: TradeHelper.getAmountsIn Infinite Loop Due to uint Underflow in Decrementing Loop Counter
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: getAmountsIn uses `for (uint i = routes.length-1; i >= 0; i--)` where i is uint256. When i reaches 0, the decrement i-- causes underflow to type(uint256).max (Solidity 0.8.13 unchecked in for-loop post-condition). The condition `i >= 0` is always true for unsigned integers, creating an infinite loop that exhausts all gas.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a well-known pattern bug with unsigned integer decrementing loops. The function is completely non-functional for any input, rendering reverse-path amount calculations impossible for all integrators.

proof_of_signal.pattern: for (uint i = routes.length-1; i >= 0; i--) -- uint underflow when i=0, i-- wraps to max; i >= 0 always true

preconditions: Any call to getAmountsIn with valid routes

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Loop never terminates; always consumes all gas
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Should use explicit break condition or different loop structure

analysis_questions: Is getAmountsIn called by any on-chain contract in the protocol?
Are there off-chain integrators relying on this function?
Does this block any critical protocol operation?
validation_requirements: Call getAmountsIn with any valid route and confirm out-of-gas revert
Verify uint decrement behavior at i=0 in Solidity 0.8.13

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Any call to getAmountsIn always reverts with out-of-gas. Function is completely non-functional.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Gas loss on every call; broken reverse routing for integrators

validation_result.impact_summary: The getAmountsIn function always reverts, rendering it completely non-functional for any integrator relying on reverse-path amount calculations.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The `getAmountsIn` function in `TradeHelper.sol` uses a `for` loop with a `uint` (unsigned integer) counter `i` that decrements (`i--`) and checks if `i >= 0`. Because `i` is an unsigned integer, the condition `i >= 0` is always true. In Solidity versions prior to 0.8.0, this would cause an infinite loop as `i` would underflow to `type(uint256).max`. However, in Solidity 0.8.13 (the version used by this contract), arithmetic operations are checked by default. Therefore, when `i` reaches 0, the `i--` operation will revert with a Panic(0x11) (Arithmetic underflow) error. Consequently, the function will always revert for any valid input and can never successfully return a result, making it completely broken.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The provided code evidence accurately points to the defective loop in `TradeHelper.sol`. While the hypothesis incorrectly assumes the underflow is unchecked and causes an infinite loop, the core defect (uint underflow on decrement causing the function to fail) is present and confirmed. The function is guaranteed to revert.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The loop condition `i >= 0` is always true for a `uint`. When `i` is 0, the post-condition `i--` will attempt to decrement 0, which causes an arithmetic underflow. In Solidity 0.8.13, this results in a guaranteed revert (Panic 0x11), making the function completely unusable.

execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: When `getAmountsIn` is called with a valid `routes` array, the loop initializes `i` to `routes.length - 1`. The loop executes normally until `i` reaches 0. After the iteration where `i == 0`, the loop attempts to execute `i--`. Since `i` is an unsigned integer, `0 - 1` causes an arithmetic underflow, which in Solidity 0.8+ triggers a revert. Thus, the function always reverts.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario is validated as a Denial of Service (DoS) / broken functionality. Any contract or off-chain integrator calling `getAmountsIn` will consistently receive a revert. While it does not exhaust all gas (as it reverts rather than infinite loops), the impact is the same: the function is entirely non-functional.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Integrators relying on `TradeHelper.getAmountsIn` to calculate required input amounts for a desired output will be unable to do so. The function is permanently broken, requiring a contract upgrade or redeployment to fix.
recommended_mitigations: Change the loop counter to a signed integer (e.g., `int256`) and cast it to `uint256` when accessing arrays: `for (int256 i = int256(routes.length - 1); i >= 0; i--) { ... amounts[uint256(i)] ... }`
Alternatively, keep `uint` but change the loop condition and decrement: `for (uint i = routes.length; i > 0; i--) { ... amounts[i-1] ... }`
validation_result.replication_plan: Deploy `TradeHelper` and call `getAmountsIn` with any valid `amountOut` and a `route` array of length >= 1. Observe the transaction reverting with a Panic(0x11) arithmetic underflow error.
